title: Dendrochilum tenue
intro: Dendrochilum tenue, a species of orchid, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical regions of Asia. Traditionally, Dendrochilum tenue has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction and the alleviation of respiratory problems, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents isolated from the plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dendrochilum tenue is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Dendrochilum tenue is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Aeridinae. The genus Dendrochilum is part of the tribe Aeridinae, which is a group of orchids characterized by their epiphytic growth habit.
